hypothesis: A Transformer encoder–decoder employing 150 cm⁻¹ patch-based spectrum embedding and a jointly trained spectral-alignment reconstruction network (optimized with combined L_S2S + λL_reconstruction loss), pretrained on ~790,000 simulated spectra and fine-tuned on NIST spectra, will more accurately identify molecular components from IR mixture spectra (including imbalanced and ternary mixtures)—as measured by higher Top-1/5/10 accuracy and Tanimoto similarity—than model variants lacking patch-based embedding or the reconstruction alignment.
